text: <par>None of these final glottal stops are 'real' consonants, they are Jones' way of registering the final creakiness that is a feature of this tone.</par>
<par>Pwo final glottal stops indicate creaky voice, a feature of the respective tone.</par>
<par>M1: s or p? Also: delete second hyphen? Or is there an Etym. <latinform>wa</latinform> (see p. 4)?</par>
<par>sha = 66 or 292, or perh. a BP p?,cf. below; M2: 457? or b?</par>
<par><latinform>a</latinform> = BP prefx? M2: prob. not #288 b-rang</par>
<par>BP pfx? or reduced from 315? M2: 315 or 2102? M3: 8?</par>
<par>1382? also, here <latinform>ùl bop</latinform> to correlate with <latinform>ùl buk</latinform>--can it? or are both just semant. null sffxs? = 164??</par>
<par>1382? Or 1388? (458?) And cf. Pattani2,3; Lotha (p. 7 end)</par>
<par>M1: 2104? if so, add LIVER to gloss? M2: da = 1382?</par>
<par>M1: ;BP pfx? or 229? 1390? or 66? (presume <latinform>(k)a</latinform> because of <latinform>ʔə</latinform>, etc.)</par>
<par>M1: 1390 (on basis of previous) or just p?</par>
<par>The variant with medial <latinform>-l-</latinform> might be due to confusion with the medial in <xref ref="5636">#5636</xref>.</par>
<par>cf. ? STOMACH 7.6.1. etc. M3: 189 WATER? (or 16 BODY PART)</par>
<par>The word-family relationship between WT <latinform>rum</latinform> 'womb' and <latinform>sbrum-pa</latinform> 'pregnant' was already recognized by W. Simon (1975:250).</par>
<par>Karbi <latinform>hem</latinform> has nothing to do with <xref ref="670">#670</xref>, contra VSTB, pp. 226-7.</par>
<par>Contra Löffler 1966, I do not assign the Lushai (Mizo) and Mru forms to <xref ref="661">#661</xref>.</par>
